violations.text: The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/07/2023

Food was observed held at 50-58 degrees Fahrenheit in the refrigerator. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in there for more than 4 hours. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). The refrigerator had an ambient air temperature of 52 degrees Fahrenheit. Refrigerator was taken out of service during time of inspection and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/09/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/09/2023

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cream) in the refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as cream during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2023

Cheese and hot dogs were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Per person in charge, pest control services will be contacted. New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2023
Single-service (takeout containers) were observed stored on the floor. Single-service items must be stored at least 6 inches off the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2023
